figures.caption: Figure 1: Illustration of the failure modes of traditional pruning methods on spatio-temporal driving inputs and the corresponding improvements achieved by ST-Prune.

Figure 4: Qualitative visualization of ST-Prune on a challenging grounding scenario from NuInstruct. (a) Overall pruning results across all six camera views and five consecutive frames (T-4 to Current), comparing raw input, DivPrune, and the two stages of ST-Prune (MTP and RSP). The red bounding box highlights the target vehicle in the Back Right camera at the current timestep. (b) Detailed token retention patterns zoomed into the Back Right view at the current moment (c) Responses from different pruning methods to the grounding query, where only ST-Prune correctly identifies the target camera and produces accurate bounding box coordinates.

Figure 2: The ST-Prune pipeline for spatio-temporal token reduction. The framework processes multi-view, multi-frame inputs through a dual-stage selection process: (1) MTP, which targets temporal redundancy using motion and recency priors, and (2) RSP, which addresses spatial redundancy across the multi-view spatial geometry. Each stage leverages an iterative Max-Min Selection engine to maintain a diverse and semantically rich token set by optimizing a diversity objective augmented with corresponding temporal or spatial weighted scores.
